programmes.show_description: Harry wants a memorial tattoo dedicated to a very intimate part of himself after surviving testicular cancer. Nicole has a portrait that resembles armpit hair.
The contestants argue about facial hair. Mike Wozniak vaults a high fence. And Lee Mack struggles to see a balloon.
Jimmy Carr hosts as Sean Lock and Miles Jupp take on Jon Richardson and Rose Matafeo. Vic Reeves joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With Rachel Riley.
Teleshopping
Teleshopping
Paul Quentel, the president of Alfred Angelo, the second-largest bridal retailer in the USA, poses as a reality TV contestant to go undercover in his own company.
Mike Bloom, the president and COO of discount chain Family Dollar, goes incognito to find out where the firm is excelling and where it needs improvement.
In Devon, there's tension at Frankie and Jason's table, Kate and Ian entertain their guests with a bird of prey, and a surprise discovery could make Chris and Amy's meal hard to stomach...
In Leicester, there's food inspired by luxury cruises, a moving musical performance that reduces one guest to tears, some outspoken comments and a serving of army-style 'banter'.
...Selling: Firefighter Ryan and Lesley are ready to set their property apart. But as Jonathan tackles turf trouble, will Drew be too busy with a firefighter fitness challenge?
The One with the Leak: Yanis wants his big day to be a towering success, featuring the Eiffel Tower. But when the wedding plans are leaked, the couple are left facing the consequences.
The One at a Rugby Match: Rugby-loving Wes plans the biggest wedding ever, in front of 26,000 people at a rugby match. But bride-to-be Charlotte wants something intimate.
John Hartmann, a former FBI agent and the president and CEO of Chicago-based hardware co-operative True Value, goes incognito among his workforce.
Bill Peduto, the 60th mayor of Pittsburgh, goes incognito among his workforce, to hear from the city's employees and ensure that the changes he's introduced have been effective.
Beachfront Inn & Inlet: Gordon Ramsay visits a 'Caribbean-style' resort hotel in Fort Pierce, Florida, where guests face dusty rooms, late-night concerts and poor service.
Landoll's Mohican Castle: Gordon Ramsay visits a custom-built, multi-million-dollar hotel in Loudonville, Ohio. Can he help the struggling business and its overworked owner?
...and Selling: Brand new series: Jeremy and Dayna need some real estate therapy. As the brothers dish out the diagnoses, Dayna realises she might not be ready to move.
Fraught Mess: A group of friends hop onboard looking for luxury. But will they get it? Chef Cloyce struggles with a seven-course dinner and difficult diners.
Lee Mack attempts an audacious hammer throw, Sarah Kendall makes sitting down memorable, and Jamali Maddix forgets his passport.
Jimmy Carr hosts as Sean Lock and Joe Wilkinson take on Jon Richardson and Sara Pascoe. Sam Simmons joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With Rachel Riley.
Jimmy Carr hosts as Sean Lock and Rob Beckett take on guest team captain Alan Carr and Sarah Millican. Nick Mohammed joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With Rachel Riley.
University Challenge: Mark blurs the line between enthusiastic new boyfriend and stalker. Jeremy joins a new band, but insists that he is neither a roadie nor a Bez.
The Man Show: Mark becomes depressed over Sophie living with Jeff, but Jeremy is not sure that self-mutilation is the answer. Besides, Jeremy and Jeff are getting on really well.
The artists cover Zoe's tattoo dedicated to her favourite food, Sean's lewd and lengthy leprechaun that his wife wants rid of and Chantal's pitiful pop portrait.
Lee Mack attempts an audacious hammer throw, Sarah Kendall makes sitting down memorable, and Jamali Maddix forgets his passport.
Jimmy Carr hosts as Sean Lock and Joe Wilkinson take on Jon Richardson and Sara Pascoe. Sam Simmons joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With Rachel Riley.
Teleshopping
Teleshopping
Travis Boersma, co-founder and president of US drive-through coffee shop chain Dutch Bros, does the daily grind undercover alongside his staff.
Ex-undercover boss Eric Casaburi brings together six participants in the series who were 'busted', 'nearly busted' or 'self-busted' while working in disguise among their staff.
In Leeds, the competition features a Pavarotti-style vocal performance, home-cooked fare and fine dining, a problem preparing duck to order, and a mankini...
In Bristol, the table talk ranges from romantic meetings to naked gardening, and the purple mash is more like Play-Doh, as Colin and Mandy, PJ and Owen, and joker Geoff and Gemma compete.
...Selling: Grab your Tamagotchi's and oversized flannel tees, because Craig and Simone's home is stuck in the 90s. Can the twins drag the property back into the 21st century?
The One on a Train: Self-conscious groom Richard plans a first-class trip for his bride. Can he meet straight-talking Latrice's big hopes for a deluxe event?
The Most Romantic One Ever: Groom Ash wants to pull off the most romantic wedding ever, but his bride-to-be Vicki shuns anything corny.
Jose Costa is the president of top automotive care franchise Maaco, based in North Carolina. He dons a disguise to meet the staff who keep his firm on the road to success.
Gina Rivera is the president of Phenix Salon Suites, which rents boutiques to hairdressers. She goes incognito among her franchisees to see how the company could be improved.
Peter's: Gordon visits Peter's Italian Restaurant in New York, where the peace of Long Island is shattered by the antics of the volatile Pellegrino family who run the place.
The Mixing Bowl: Bijou New York diner The Mixing Bowl is haemorrhaging money thanks to manager Mike's out-of-control marketing plans. Can Gordon get things back on track?
...and Selling: Brand new series: Looks can be deceiving with Anthony and Rachel's home but if you dig a little deeper, the cracks start to show...
Mid-Strife Crisis: The boat is doused in glam as a drag queen duo come aboard. But will it be a show-stopping stay or will the team tank on the big stage?
Charlotte Ritchie climbs a ladder with some loo roll, Mike Wozniak tries out a new catchphrase, and Lee Mack terrifies an innocent member of the public.
Jimmy Carr hosts as Sean Lock and Katherine Ryan take on Jon Richardson and Joe Wilkinson. Adam Riches as Sean Bean joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With Rachel Riley.
Jimmy Carr hosts as Sean Lock and Harriet Kemsley take on guest team captain Alan Carr and Catherine Tate. Adam Buxton joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With Rachel Riley.
Wedding: Jeremy and Nancy (Rachel Blanchard) are getting married, for visa reasons. Mark, determined to save his friend, will do whatever it takes.
Mugging: Some muggers steal Mark's BlackBerry, which puts him off his stroke with Sophie. Meanwhile, Jeremy faces a tricky dilemma over the love of his life and a threesome.
